pages.full_text:
__BROOKHOUSE FARM__

£80,000 per yr.

Grapes
3000 bottles per year

Apples
800 tons

Muller - ¼  Solaris ¾ acre
Cabinet ¾
2 or 3 bottles per vine  3000 litres
3 tons grapes
19 tons per acre
40 or 60 acres

[map annotation:] Toby &
Ali[?ce]
pages.notes: Page includes a printed OS map (glued in or photocopied) covering the Avenbury/Brookhouse Farm area, with place names visible on the map including Bythan, King [?Bungalow], Avenbury, The Old Vicarage, Avenbury Court. Handwritten notes appear to be facts about Brookhouse Farm vineyard/orchard operation — production figures, grape varieties. 'Cabinet' could be 'Cabernet' (grape variety). 'Muller' likely refers to Muller-Thurgau (white wine grape). 'Toby & Ali[ce?]' written in blue on the map portion pointing to a location. No handwritten page number visible on this page. No date evident on this page; year cannot be established from content alone.

pages.full_text:
VINEYARD [arrow pointing to vineyard rows on map]

Jon's [?dome] [?done]

Mike's workshop.
pages.notes: Page is dominated by a printed OS map of the Brookhouse Farm/vineyard area with blue handwritten annotations. 'VINEYARD' written in large blue letters with an arrow pointing to rows of vines drawn on the map. 'Jon's [?dome/done]' — second word unclear, could be 'dome' or 'done'. 'Mike's workshop.' with a line pointing to a building on the map. Handwritten page number '2' visible in top-right corner. Place names from the printed map include: The Hyde, Brookhouse Farm, Brookhouse Wood, Upper Venn Farm. Bottom-right area shows what may read '[?Bitch] Hill' — partially obscured. The vineyard rows are shown as blue parallel lines on the map. No date visible; year cannot be established from content alone.
